question: After the MoE-routing analogy, why is the current pair-slot compressed-dKV reducer still too slow, and what exact primitive is missing?
short_answer: The pair-slot route-grid kernel already uses compact route metadata and avoids the dense active_group x selected_K route matrix, but it still performs one local-table scalar accumulation per selected dKV contribution. It moves the 805M scalar many-to-one update from global atomics into a CTA-local group_acc table, then emits reduced global rows. The missing primitive is a register/lane/warp/block segmented reduction that combines selected contributions before they touch a dense local table.

measured_pairslot_evidence.read: The contribution math alone is already close to the retained sparse-atomic wall. Adding pair-slot local-table reduction doubles the S4096 dKV slice even though it cuts modeled global writes by 23.8x.

what_it_fixed: No dense active_group x selected_K route matrix.
Reduced final global writes from raw selected rows to compact active groups per tile.
what_it_did_not_fix: Every valid selected contribution still writes D16 scalars into group_acc.
The local table is initialized and emitted over active_count x D16 for every route tile and D block.
Duplicates are reduced only after scalar local-table updates, not before them.
mechanism.why_moe_style_expert_major_is_not_enough: MoE expert-major compute works because each routed expert owns heavy GEMMs. Sparse dKV has huge topk and thin per-route vector contributions; the fast reuse is token/query-major. The reducer ownership must be group-aware, but the contribution producer should remain query-major.

reject: Retrying compact pair-slot local tables with more block-shape sweeps.
Treating reduced global atomic count as sufficient if the kernel still performs one local-table update per selected contribution.
Moving compressed group to the outer contribution loop without preserving token-local Q/dO reuse.
retain: Pair-slot or equivalent compact route metadata.
Compact final reduce-by-key or reduced global writeback.
next_kernel_contract: Produce KxD contribution tiles in the current query-major selected-K schedule.
Within the producer, group selected rows by compressed group using sparse keys, not a dense active-group route matrix.
Use lane/warp/block segmented reduction to combine duplicate group rows before writing either global dKV or compact partial rows.
Avoid per-contribution local atomic/add into an active_group x D table.
decision.promotion_gate: At S4096 layer 2, producer plus segmented reduction plus final compact reducer must beat the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic dKV wall; otherwise it is not a sparse-attention backward speedup candidate.
